title: Q does not suck
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by Rommel » Sat Jun 21, 2014 1:32 pm Bagheera wrote: That's because Anno has talent and the neckbeards don't. Also, the neckbeards are misogynistic assholes, while Anno isn't. So that helps.

[]:             Quote Post by El Squibbonator » Sat Jun 21, 2014 9:19 pm Rommel wrote: >3.0 >talent

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Sat Jun 21, 2014 9:25 pm ^ I really liked 3.0 as well, I'm not gonna lie, I really liked Shinji seeing the consequences of his actions.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jun 21, 2014 9:32 pm Q was brilliant. Heavy handed at times, but brilliant nonetheless. It turned expectations on their head and got everyone talking, can't ask for more than that.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Sat Jun 21, 2014 9:37 pm ^ Indeed it was Good Sir.

[]:             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Mon Jun 23, 2014 1:16 pm I was a bit disappointed in Q in that we only see a few characters - namely Shinji, Kaworu and Rei - elaborated upon. I feel that there was a missed opportunity to build up Asuka, Mari, Misato and the Wille crew, Gendo and Fuyutsuki.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Mon Jun 23, 2014 1:21 pm Bagheera wrote: Q was brilliant. Heavy handed at times, but brilliant nonetheless. It turned expectations on their head and got everyone talking, can't ask for more than that.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Jun 23, 2014 1:26 pm Bickering? Yes, I guess so - but note that for every pessimist about how things have gone and will go there is also another optimist. No one, even the most gloomy of us (you know who you are...) can convincingly argue that they must be right, because there are just too many people who think differently. It's the uncertainty that's the bummer!

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Mon Jun 23, 2014 1:30 pm ^ Exactly. It's this uncertainty that makes it so maddening of a film. The ending could go either way. But, I guess that's the point. That's what makes it so compelling.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jun 23, 2014 1:38 pm xanderkh wrote: True, but has that been a GOOD thing?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Jun 23, 2014 1:42 pm driftking18594 wrote: I was a bit disappointed in Q in that we only see a few characters - namely Shinji, Kaworu and Rei - elaborated upon. I feel that there was a missed opportunity to build up Asuka, Mari, Misato and the Wille crew, Gendo and Fuyutsuki.

[]:             Quote Post by Rommel » Mon Jun 23, 2014 4:01 pm Bagheera wrote: Q was brilliant. Heavy handed at times, but brilliant nonetheless. It turned expectations on their head and got everyone talking, can't ask for more than that.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jun 23, 2014 4:08 pm Rommel wrote: It was crap. Why bother doing an Eva movie when it has nothing to do with Eva and when you are going to ignore everything? Oh, Asuka goes through life-changing trauma. Lets completely ignore that. Haven't explained who Mari is, oh wait there's no plan for her. Oh, and flying battleships! Fuck yeah! Poor characterization to the point where the characters are unrecognizable and nonsensical plot are exactly the hallmark of bad fanfiction. If that's the standard for people who do it for free, why does Anno get a pass? anyway, that's just my opinion, but calling it brilliant is ridiculous.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Jun 23, 2014 4:33 pm Rommel wrote: Why bother doing an Eva movie when it has nothing to do with Eva and when you are going to ignore everything?

[]:             Quote Post by Rommel » Mon Jun 23, 2014 4:34 pm There's a different between not answering question and bad storytelling. Empire Strikes Back did a great job at the former. Shoving in a Timeskip (TM) because you have no idea what to do with your story is pretty good proof of how bad this actually is. And that's the worst of it, because you can so obviously tell they had no idea what they were doing. Hell, the fact three movies in it and no one has a clue what's going on, and that every movie seems totally alien to one before is also bad storytelling. I've said this before: make a list of things that make a bad fic. Q meets damn near all of them. PS: Oh, yes we were told it was different from the beginning, with a near shot for shot recreation of the first 5 episodes. Totally different. And if he's 'done' Eva then not do something original? Why waste his time and ours on this?

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Mon Jun 23, 2014 4:43 pm ^ Anno is a brand. No matter what he does and what you say, everything will be countered with "2 deep 4 u" philosophy. Seriously, Anno could take a dump in a bucket and slap some freudian quotes on it, and it would be considered deep art. Because its Anno. Criticising him is like heresy here.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Jun 23, 2014 4:47 pm 2 deep 4 u? Speak for yourself - you can find plenty of explanations around here if you're prepared to take them on board.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Mon Jun 23, 2014 4:50 pm Can an Admin cut and paste this into the rebuild section to get us back on topic please?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jun 23, 2014 5:29 pm Rommel wrote: There's a different between not answering question and bad storytelling. Empire Strikes Back did a great job at the former. Shoving in a Timeskip (TM) because you have no idea what to do with your story is pretty good proof of how bad this actually is. And that's the worst of it, because you can so obviously tell they had no idea what they were doing.

[]:             Quote Post by Nepheliad » Mon Jun 23, 2014 5:56 pm driftking18594 wrote: I was a bit disappointed in Q in that we only see a few characters - namely Shinji, Kaworu and Rei - elaborated upon. I feel that there was a missed opportunity to build up Asuka, Mari, Misato and the Wille crew, Gendo and Fuyutsuki .
